# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

```typescript
import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.142.0/http/server.ts";

const server = serve({ port: 8080 });
console.log("HTTP web server started at http://localhost:8080/");

for await (const req of server) {
  const url = new URL(req.request.url);
  
  // Get module path from environment variables or query parameters
  let modulePath = url.searchParams.get("module") || Deno.env.get("MODULE_PATH");
  
  if (modulePath) {
    try {
      const module = await import(`file://${Deno.cwd()}/${modulePath}`);
      
      // Send response back to client with loaded module's content
      req.respond({ body: JSON.stringify(module) });
   <｜begin▁of▁sentence｜>//
